title: ‘No Kings’ protest: What protesters said and why they showed up
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Thousands gathered on Boston Common for the 'No Kings' protest, one of over 160 rallies in Massachusetts.
Speakers included politicians like Gov. Maura Healey and Sen. Elizabeth Warren; performances featured the Dropkick Murphys.
Protesters, including veterans and church groups, expressed concerns over democracy's future and opposed monarchical power.

title: Photo scroll: Scenes from Boston’s ‘No Kings’ protest on March 28
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Thousands gathered on Boston Common for the 'No Kings' protest, part of 160+ events across Massachusetts.
Urban League of Eastern Massachusetts president Rahsaan Hall and Sen. Elizabeth Warren addressed the Boston rally.
The protest was organized amid wider 'No Kings' demonstrations in Europe and other parts of the U.S.

title: Photos: ‘No Kings’ sparks protests in Europe
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Thousands participate in 'No Kings' protests across Europe on March 28, 2026, expressing anti-Donald Trump sentiments.
'No Kings' rallies occur in France, Italy, Spain, and the Netherlands, with demonstrators opposing Trump's policies.
Over 3,000 protests are planned in the U.S., with Massachusetts hosting over 160 events, second to California.

title: Bravo Just Dropped the first full “Real Housewives of Rhode Island” Trailer. Here’s everything we learned.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres April 2 at 9 p.m. on Bravo, the first New England-based series.
Alicia Carmody, part of the cast, co-owns Pizza Mamma in Cranston and has deep family ties in Rhode Island.
Liz McGraw, another cast member, owns a cannabis dispensary in Providence and lives in a waterfront mansion.

title: Hampden County Sheriff plans dozens of job cuts
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hampden County Sheriff Nick Cocchi announces about 50 job cuts to save $4 million due to budget reductions.
The department faces a $26.5 million budget cut for fiscal year 2026, affecting patrols and specialized units.
Sheriff Cocchi cites long-term structural funding issues dating back to the 1999 abolition of county government.

title: Quincy approves money for new firefighting gear after PFAS found in old gear
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy city council approved $2.65 million for new PFAS-free firefighting gear for over 270 firefighters.
The decision follows testing that revealed current gear contains PFAS, linked to cancer and other health risks.
Massachusetts law will ban sale of firefighting gear with intentionally added PFAS starting January 2026.

title: With his new doc  ‘Henry David Thoreau,’ Don Henley returns to Walden
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Don Henley co-produced 'Henry David Thoreau' documentary, premiering March 30 and 31 on PBS, exploring Thoreau's life and legacy.
Henley founded The Walden Woods Project in 1990 to preserve Thoreau's land in response to planned commercial development.
Documentary features narrations by George Clooney, Jeff Goldblum, Ted Danson, and Meryl Streep, and includes insights from modern thinkers.

title: World Cup friendly match at Gillette was an ‘amazing’ off-day field trip for the Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Celtics attended a friendly soccer match between France and Brazil at Gillette Stadium.
Kylian Mbappe scored, leading France to a 2-1 victory over Brazil during the match.
Celtics rookie Hugo Gonzalez swapped jerseys with Mbappe and felt they shared mutual respect.

title: Payton Pritchard’s huge night leads short-handed Celtics over streaking Hawks: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Payton Pritchard led the Celtics with 36 points, helping the team overcome the Atlanta Hawks 109-102.
With Jaylen Brown sidelined, Jayson Tatum contributed 25 points in 36 minutes, despite shooting difficulties.
Jordan Walsh capitalized on increased playtime, securing three blocks and five points, enhancing the Celtics' defense.

title: Why did Jayson Tatum come back this season? The Celtics’ success had ‘a lot’ to do with it.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics 10 months post-surgery, motivated by the team's strong championship contention.
Tatum has gradually increased his playtime, now averaging 9.2 rebounds per game, despite shooting below career averages.
The Celtics are currently in second place, poised for a potential home playoff series as the season's end approaches.
